component.name: Mackes XFCE Workstation
component.summary: Unified XFCE workstation: panel, dock, settings, mesh networking, fleet management

description.p: Mackes XFCE Workstation replaces xfce4-panel, xfdesktop, and the stock settings manager with a unified shell that ships its own top status bar, macOS-style bottom dock, and a sidebar workbench for every setting Mackes manages. Underneath it stays a standard XFCE session — xfwm4 or i3 (toggleable) for window management, LightDM for login, Plymouth for boot.

ul.li: Rust-native top bar + bottom dock (single mackes-panel binary) with Plank-parity dock behavior: pinned launchers, window grouping, multi-window indicators, right-click context menus
Live status cluster (mesh peers, clipboard items, volume, battery, unread notifications, sessions) refreshed every 2 s
Apple-menu launcher with About / Settings / Software Update / Recent Items / Applications submenu (8 buckets) / Force Quit / Sleep / Restart / Shut Down / Lock / Sign Out
First-run setup wizard with curated presets (hashbang, mackes, daylight, vanilla)
Notification Drawer with live data for Mesh, Bluetooth, Volume, Brightness, Battery, Hardware
Mesh networking via Headscale + Tailscale — peers, shares, services as first-class dock items
Remote desktop integration with auto-discovered SSH / RDP / VNC peers via Remmina
Bidirectional clipboard sync across mesh peers
Fleet management via Ansible-pull with curated playbooks
Optional i3 tiling window manager via mackes-wm switcher (Workbench → System → Window Manager)
System maintenance: snapshots, drift detection, system updates, dependency checks
PatternFly v6 design tokens, Red Hat Display/Text/Mono fonts, Mackes-Carbon symbolic icon theme

description.p: Win10 layout reskin. Single bottom taskbar replaces the top bar + Plank dock. Right-click Start opens a 9-item Fedora admin menu (Root Terminal / DNF / journalctl / systemctl / SELinux / firewall / disk-clean) in terminator with the shell kept open. Win10-style desktop watermark shows in the lower-right when DNF updates are pending (4 h poll). Status-cluster click toggles the Workbench closed on a repeated press. XDG user-dirs remapped to mesh-sync targets (Music/Pictures/Videos/Documents to ~/QNM-Mesh/, Downloads stays local). XFCE menu hides expanded to every replaced component + propagated to existing users on every login.

First-boot hotfix. The mackes-enforce-session autostart converges every login onto i3 + mackes-panel (no xfwm4, no xfce4-panel, no xfdesktop). WorkbenchWindow WM_CLASS pinned to Mackes-shell + i3 float rule. Status-cluster icons open the Workbench focused on the relevant panel via mackes --focus <slug>.

Plank-parity dock with pinned launchers, tasklist, and window grouping. Live status cluster (icon + number, 2 s poll, opens drawer on click). Optional i3 tiling WM via mackes-wm switcher. About Mackes window. Drawer probes now wired to live data sources (pactl, bluetoothctl, tailscale, MPRIS, UPower). The legacy 1.6.7 release on the 2.x line is intentionally omitted from this history; that build pre-dates the 1.0.x reset.
First-boot panel polish bundle: visible top-bar icons, auto-sizing dock, 12-hour clock with weather popover, status-cluster review popovers, _NET_WM_STRUT_PARTIAL on panel and dock.

Mackes XFCE Workstation: full pivot from Mackes Shell 2.x. Replaces xfce4-panel + xfdesktop with a unified Rust panel (top bar + bottom dock + wallpaper). PatternFly v6 design tokens and Red Hat font family throughout. Workspaces collapsed to one (app-switching replaces virtual desktops). LightDM + Plymouth + apple-menu chrome rebuilt to match the new design language. Performance gates: cold start < 200 ms, idle CPU < 1%, RSS <= 150 MB.
